sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section II, List of Answers on Topics 81:1

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section II, List of Answers on Topics 81:1
items.html: In the ten Sefirot of reflected light, in which Malchut is regarded as the root and the Keter (See answer 148), all the parts of reflected light that clothe her upper ones pass through her from below upward. For this reason, every lower one is regarded as containing all the parts of reflected light that belong to the Sefirot above it.
